nodes.title: Cancel every active timeout
nodes.why: clearAllTimeouts should be a full reset. We loop over all saved handles, clear each one, then empty the registry.
nodes.alternative: Replace the map with a new one without clearing old handles.
nodes.tradeoff: Looping every handle is O(k), but it guarantees no old timeout still fires later.

nodes.title: Render null/undefined as empty text
nodes.why: This keeps the renderer safe when children include null values and avoids crashes.
nodes.alternative: Return null and make callers handle it.
nodes.tradeoff: Returning a text node keeps one consistent return type, but creates an extra empty node.

nodes.title: Convert primitive children to text nodes
nodes.why: String and number children are common. Converting them directly to text nodes makes rendering predictable.
nodes.tradeoff: More flexible input handling, with a small extra type-check branch.

nodes.title: Skip null props and map className
nodes.why: `className` is a common JS prop name, but DOM attributes use `class`. We also skip null props to avoid noisy attributes.
nodes.alternative: Set every key directly with no filtering.
nodes.tradeoff: A little more logic, but cleaner DOM output and better compatibility with common input shapes.

nodes.title: Render array children recursively
nodes.why: This handles nested trees naturally and preserves child order.
nodes.alternative: Flatten children first, then render in a second pass.
nodes.tradeoff: Direct recursion is simple and readable, but very deep trees can grow call stack usage.

nodes.title: Support non-array single child
nodes.why: Some node shapes use one child value instead of an array. This branch supports both formats.
nodes.alternative: Force callers to always pass `children` as an array.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly more branching, but much friendlier API for users.
